answer.text: Basis strengthens — spot prices rise relative to futures prices
Basis weakens — futures prices rise relative to spot prices
Both spot and futures prices fall equally

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Short hedgers are short futures + long spot. Strengthening basis means spot rises more (or falls less) than futures → net gain from the combined position. **The Trap:** Basis weakening benefits LONG hedgers. Equal movements in both markets result in zero hedge P&L. Volatility increase doesn't directly determine hedge benefit — basis direction does. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers hedge P&L analysis based on basis changes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Commodity producers (short hedgers) should monitor basis closely — strengthening basis near expiry is the ideal scenario for their hedged positions.
